zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Beaumont IV於1460年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾特利時，他的父親John Beaumont31歲，母親Elizabeth Cooke30歲。他於1489年1月10日與Johanna de Sandford在英國越可爾特利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1532年12月10日在英國英格蘭越可爾吉可亞頓享年72歲，埋葬在英國越可爾吉可亞頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Beaumont IV于1460年出生在英国英格兰越可爾特利时，他的父亲John Beaumont31岁，母亲Elizabeth Cooke30岁。他于1489年1月10日与Johanna de Sandford在英国越可爾特利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1532年12月10日在英国英格兰越可爾吉可亞頓享年72岁，埋葬在英国越可爾吉可亞頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
English (Kent): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegmund.
German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Beaumont IV was born in 1460, in Whitley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Beaumont, was 31 and his mother, Elizabeth Cooke, was 30. He married Johanna de Sandford on 10 January 1489, in Whitley, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He died on 10 December 1532, in Kirkheaton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 72, and was buried in Kirkheaton, Yorkshire, England.

Il a épousé Johanna de Sandford le 10 janvier 1489, à Whitley, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 10 décembre 1532, à Kirkheaton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Kirkheaton, Everwyck, Angleterre.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Beaumont IV è nato il nel 1460 nel Whitley, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Beaumont, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Cooke, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Johanna de Sandford il 10 gennaio 1489, nel Whitley, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Morì il 10 dicembre 1532, nel Kirkheaton, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolto a Kirkheaton, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Beaumont IVが1460年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウィトリーで生まれた時，父John Beaumontは31歳，母Elizabeth Cookeは30歳であった。1489年1月10日にイギリスヨークシュアイアウィトリーでJohanna de Sandfordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1532年12月10日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアキルクヒートンで72歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアキルクヒートンにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint John the Baptist's Church, Kirkhea: 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 요르크실르 커크히턴 스트 존 테 바프티스트'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Beaumont IV(이)가 1460년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 휘틀리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Beaumont(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Cooke(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1489년 1월 10일 영국 요르크실르 휘틀리에서 Johanna de Sandford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1532년 12월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 커크히턴에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 커크히턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Beaumont IV nasceu em 1460, em Whitley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Beaumont, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Cooke, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Johanna de Sandford em 10 de janeiro de 1489, em Whitley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 10 de dezembro de 1532, em Kirkheaton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Kirkheaton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Saint John the Baptist's Church, Kirkhea: ст джон т баптист'с чурч, кирхитон, вест еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Beaumont IV родился в 1460 г. в местности уитли, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Beaumont было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Cooke, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Johanna de Sandford 10 января 1489 г. в местности уитли, еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Он умер 10 декабря 1532 г. в местности кирхитон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности кирхитон, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Beaumont IV nació en 1460, en Whitley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Beaumont, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Cooke, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Johanna de Sandford el 10 de enero de 1489, en Whitley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas. Murió el 10 de diciembre de 1532, en Kirkheaton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultado en Kirkheaton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
